sourceMovie.title: Peter Rabbit 2: The Runaway
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Peter Rabbit 2: The Runaway (2021) stuck because it commits to its family premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Will Gluck's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The family and comedy blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Smurfs: The Lost Village
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Peter Rabbit 2: The Runaway clicked for you, Smurfs: The Lost Village (2017) is a strong next stop: both trade in family and comedy, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Peter Rabbit 2: The Runaway?
faq.answer: Try Smurfs: The Lost Village, Harold and the Purple Crayon, My Little Pony: A New Generation—each shares DNA with Peter Rabbit 2: The Runaway while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Peter Rabbit 2: The Runaway?
faq.answer: Peter Rabbit 2: The Runaway (2021) is best described as Family, Comedy, Adventure. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Peter Rabbit 2: The Runaway part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Peter Rabbit Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
